---
title: "Phylogenetic Anchor Filtering"
author: "Srishti Arya"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    df_print: paged
params:
  fasta:          "../../../trees/Nematoda_18S/Nematoda_18S_cleaned.fa"
  metadata:       "../../../trees/Nematoda_18S/Nematoda_18S_metadata.csv"
  output:         "../../../trees/Nematoda_18S/"
  distance:       "k2p"
  threshold:      0.04
  subset:         75
  min_overlap:    0
  fasttree_bin:   "/Users/srishti/miniconda3/bin/fasttree"
  drop_divergent: TRUE   # set true to remove flagged anchors before plotting/tree
  dedup:          0.02
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  message = TRUE,
  warning = TRUE
)
getwd()
```

# Setup
## Libraries

```{r libraries, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(seqinr)
library(ape)
library(phangorn)
library(optparse)
library(ggplot2)
```

## Parameters

Parameters are controlled via the `params` block in the YAML header above.
Edit them there before knitting, or pass them with `rmarkdown::render()`.

```{r params}
opt <- list(
  fasta        = params$fasta,
  metadata     = params$metadata,
  output       = params$output,
  distance     = params$distance,
  threshold    = params$threshold,
  subset       = params$subset,
  min_overlap  = params$min_overlap,
  fasttree_bin = params$fasttree_bin,
  drop_divergent = params$drop_divergent,
  dedup        = params$dedup
)

# Create output directory if needed
if (!dir.exists(opt$output)) dir.create(opt$output, recursive = TRUE)
```

# Helper Functions

## Run FastTree
```{r helper-fasttree}
run_fasttree <- function(aln, label, outdir, fasttree_bin = "FastTree") {
  tmp_aln  <- tempfile(fileext = ".fasta")
  tmp_tree <- file.path(outdir, paste0(label, "_tree.nwk"))
  tmp_log  <- file.path(outdir, paste0(label, "_fasttree.log"))

  write.phyDat(aln, file = tmp_aln, format = "fasta")

  cmd <- sprintf(
    "%s -nt -gtr -quiet %s > %s 2> %s",
    fasttree_bin, tmp_aln, tmp_tree, tmp_log
  )
  message(sprintf("  Running FastTree [%s]…", label))
  ret <- system(cmd)
  if (ret != 0) stop(sprintf("FastTree failed for [%s]. See %s", label, tmp_log))

  tree <- read.tree(tmp_tree)
  file.remove(tmp_aln)
  message(sprintf("  FastTree done [%s]: %d tips", label, length(tree$tip.label)))
  return(tree)
}
```

## Faith's PD
```{r helper-faith-pd}
faith_pd <- function(tree, tips) {
  # Prune tree to selected tips only, then sum all branch lengths
  if (length(tips) < 2) return(0)
  subtree <- keep.tip(tree, tips)
  sum(subtree$edge.length, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

# Step 1: Load Data
```{r load-data}
message(" Step 1: Loading data ")

# Load alignment
aln     <- read.phyDat("../../../trees/Nematoda_18S/Nematoda_18S_cleaned.fa", format = "fasta", type = "DNA")
all_ids <- names(aln)
message(sprintf("  Alignment: %d sequences, %d sites",
                length(all_ids), ncol(as.character(aln))))

# Load metadata
meta <- read.csv("../../../trees/Nematoda_18S/Nematoda_18S_metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
required_cols <- c("label", "type")
missing_cols  <- setdiff(required_cols, colnames(meta))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("Metadata CSV missing columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

# 'dataset' column is optional
if (!"dataset" %in% colnames(meta)) {
  meta$dataset <- NA_character_
  message("  Note: no 'dataset' column found; dataset will be NA for all tips")
}

# Validate that all FASTA IDs are present in metadata
missing_meta <- setdiff(all_ids, meta$label)
if (length(missing_meta) > 0) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "%d FASTA IDs not found in metadata and will be ignored: %s%s",
    length(missing_meta),
    paste(head(missing_meta, 5), collapse = ", "),
    if (length(missing_meta) > 5) "…" else ""
  ))
  keep_ids <- intersect(all_ids, meta$label)
  aln      <- subset(aln, keep_ids)
  all_ids  <- keep_ids
}

anchor_ids <- meta$label[meta$type == "anchor" & meta$label %in% all_ids]
sample_ids <- meta$label[meta$type == "sample" & meta$label %in% all_ids]

message(sprintf("  Anchors: %d", length(anchor_ids)))
message(sprintf("  Samples: %d", length(sample_ids)))

if (length(sample_ids) == 0) stop("No sample sequences found in metadata.")
if (length(anchor_ids) == 0) stop("No anchor sequences found in metadata.")
if (opt$subset > length(anchor_ids)) {
  stop(sprintf(
    "--subset (%d) exceeds number of anchors (%d)",
    opt$subset, length(anchor_ids)
  ))
}
```

# Step 2: Distance Matrix
```{r distance-matrix}
message(" Step 2: Computing distance matrix ")

dist_model <- if (opt$distance == "k2p") "K80" else "raw"
message(sprintf("  Model: %s", dist_model))

dnabin  <- as.DNAbin(aln)
distmat <- dist.dna(dnabin, 
                    model = dist_model, 
                    pairwise.deletion = TRUE,
                    as.matrix = TRUE)

message(sprintf("  Distance matrix: %d × %d", nrow(distmat), ncol(distmat)))

dist_path <- file.path(opt$output, "distance_matrix.csv")
write.csv(as.matrix(distmat), dist_path)
message("  Saved: distance_matrix.csv")
```

# Step 2b: remove sequences not overlapping samples
```{r overlaps}
# Optional overlap matrix
if (!is.null(opt$min_overlap)) {
  message(sprintf("  Overlap filter: >= %d shared ungapped columns", opt$min_overlap))
  aln_mat    <- as.character(as.DNAbin(aln))
  anchor_mat <- aln_mat[anchor_ids, , drop = FALSE]
  sample_mat <- aln_mat[sample_ids, , drop = FALSE]
  overlap_mat <- matrix(0L,
                        nrow     = length(anchor_ids),
                        ncol     = length(sample_ids),
                        dimnames = list(anchor_ids, sample_ids))
  for (j in seq_along(sample_ids)) {
    s_ungapped <- sample_mat[j, ] != "-" & sample_mat[j, ] != "n"
    for (i in seq_along(anchor_ids)) {
      a_ungapped <- anchor_mat[i, ] != "-" & anchor_mat[i, ] != "n"
      overlap_mat[i, j] <- sum(s_ungapped & a_ungapped)
    }
  }
  rm(aln_mat, anchor_mat, sample_mat)
} else {
  overlap_mat <- NULL
}
```


# optional stop: check alignment (can remove divergent seqs or choose to manually keep)
```{r stop-alignment-check}
message("Flagging divergent anchors ")

# For each anchor, find its MINIMUM distance to any sample
anchor_min_dist <- apply(overlap_mat[anchor_ids, sample_ids, drop = FALSE], 1, min, na.rm = TRUE)

# Flag anchors whose minimum distance to any sample exceeds a multiple of the median
DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD <- median(anchor_min_dist, na.rm = TRUE) + 
                        3 * mad(anchor_min_dist, na.rm = TRUE)  # median + 3*MAD is robust to outliers

divergent_anchors <- names(anchor_min_dist[anchor_min_dist > DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD])

message(sprintf("  Median min-dist to samples: %.4f", median(anchor_min_dist)))
message(sprintf("  Flagging threshold (median + 3*MAD): %.4f", DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD))
message(sprintf("  Divergent anchors flagged: %d", length(divergent_anchors)))
if (length(divergent_anchors) > 0) {
  message("  Flagged anchors:")
  for (a in divergent_anchors) {
    message(sprintf("    %s  (min dist to any sample = %.4f)", a, anchor_min_dist[a]))
  }
}

anchors_to_drop <- divergent_anchors # keep copy

if (isTRUE(as.logical(opt$drop_divergent)) && length(divergent_anchors) > 0) {
  message(sprintf("  drop_divergent=TRUE: removing %d flagged anchors", length(divergent_anchors)))
  anchor_ids <- setdiff(anchor_ids, divergent_anchors)
  divergent_anchors <- character(0)   # nothing to flag anymore
  anchor_min_dist   <- anchor_min_dist[anchor_ids]
}

plot_mat <- distmat[anchor_ids, sample_ids, drop = FALSE]

# Sort anchor rows by their min distance (most divergent at top)
row_order <- order(anchor_min_dist, decreasing = TRUE)
plot_mat  <- plot_mat[row_order, , drop = FALSE]

# Melt for ggplot
dist_df <- as.data.frame(plot_mat) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("anchor") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-anchor, names_to = "sample", values_to = "distance") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    anchor   = factor(anchor, levels = rownames(plot_mat)),
    flagged  = anchor %in% divergent_anchors
  )

# Heatmap
p_heatmap <- ggplot2::ggplot(dist_df, ggplot2::aes(x = sample, y = anchor, fill = distance)) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile() +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1, name = "Distance") +
  ggplot2::scale_y_discrete(
    labels = function(x) ifelse(x %in% divergent_anchors, paste0("*** ", x), x)
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 7) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.x  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = ggplot2::element_text(
      colour = ifelse(levels(dist_df$anchor) %in% divergent_anchors, "red", "black"),
      size   = 5
    ),
    panel.grid   = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title    = "Anchor–sample distance matrix",
    subtitle = sprintf("*** flagged (min dist > %.4f = median + 3×MAD); n=%d flagged",
                       DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD, length(divergent_anchors)),
    x        = "Samples", y = "Anchors (sorted by min distance to any sample)"
  )

print(p_heatmap)

# Also save to file
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path(opt$output, "divergence_heatmap.pdf"),
                p_heatmap, width = 10, height = max(4, length(anchor_ids) * 0.15),
                limitsize = FALSE)
message("  Saved: divergence_heatmap.png")

message(" Step 2b: Manual alignment check — PAUSED ")

message(sprintf(
  "  Alignment written to: %s",
  file.path(opt$output, "final_subset_aligned.fasta")
))
message("  Please inspect and edit the alignment now (e.g. fix reverse-complemented sequences).")
message("  When done, save your edited file to:")
message(sprintf("  %s", file.path(opt$output, "alignment_edited.fasta")))
message("  Then re-run from Step 3 onwards.")

# Write out current alignment for manual inspection
write.FASTA(as.DNAbin(aln), file.path(opt$output, "alignment_for_inspection.fasta"))

if (isTRUE(as.logical(opt$drop_divergent))) {
  # Auto-remove divergent anchors from the alignment and proceed
  keep_ids <- setdiff(names(aln), anchors_to_drop)
  aln      <- subset(aln, keep_ids)
  all_ids  <- names(aln)
  anchor_ids <- intersect(anchor_ids, all_ids)
  sample_ids <- intersect(sample_ids, all_ids)
  message(sprintf("  drop_divergent=TRUE: alignment trimmed to %d sequences, proceeding automatically.", length(all_ids)))

} else {
  message(" Step 2b: Manual alignment check — PAUSED ")
  message(sprintf("  Alignment written to: %s", file.path(opt$output, "alignment_for_inspection.fasta")))
  message("  Please inspect and edit the alignment now (e.g. fix reverse-complemented sequences).")
  message("  When done, save your edited file to:")
  message(sprintf("  %s", file.path(opt$output, "alignment_edited.fasta")))
  message("  Then re-run from Step 3 onwards.")

  edited_path <- file.path(opt$output, "alignment_edited.fasta")
  if (!file.exists(edited_path)) {
    stop(
      "No edited alignment found at: ", edited_path, "\n",
      "  Edit the alignment and save it there, then re-knit/re-run from this chunk."
    )
  }

  message("  Found edited alignment — loading and replacing aln.")
  aln     <- read.phyDat(edited_path, format = "fasta", type = "DNA")
  all_ids <- names(aln)

  missing_after_edit <- setdiff(c(anchor_ids, sample_ids), all_ids)
  if (length(missing_after_edit) > 0) {
    warning(sprintf(
      "%d sequences present before editing are missing from edited alignment: %s%s",
      length(missing_after_edit),
      paste(head(missing_after_edit, 5), collapse = ", "),
      if (length(missing_after_edit) > 5) "…" else ""
    ))
    anchor_ids <- intersect(anchor_ids, all_ids)
    sample_ids <- intersect(sample_ids, all_ids)
  }
  message(sprintf("  Edited alignment loaded: %d sequences", length(all_ids)))
}
```


# Step 3: Whitelist Anchors
Filter anchors by distance threshold
```{r whitelist-anchors}
message(sprintf(" Step 3: Flagging anchors by distance threshold (threshold = %.4f) ",
                opt$threshold))

anchor_sample_dist <- distmat[anchor_ids, sample_ids, drop = FALSE]

# Flag: anchor is whitelisted if ANY sample satisfies distance (+ overlap) criteria
whitelisted <- character(0)

for (anchor in anchor_ids) {
  for (sample in sample_ids) {
    d <- anchor_sample_dist[anchor, sample]
    if (is.na(d)) next
    passes_dist    <- d <= opt$threshold
    passes_overlap <- if (!is.null(overlap_mat)) {
      overlap_mat[anchor, sample] >= opt$min_overlap
    } else {
      TRUE
    }
    if (passes_dist && passes_overlap) {
      whitelisted <- c(whitelisted, anchor)
      break
    }
  }
}

non_whitelisted <- setdiff(anchor_ids, whitelisted)

message(sprintf("  Anchors passing threshold (whitelisted): %d / %d",
                length(whitelisted), length(anchor_ids)))
message(sprintf("  Anchors outside threshold (non-whitelisted, kept): %d / %d",
                length(non_whitelisted), length(anchor_ids)))

# No hard stop — all anchors proceed to PD step
```

# de-dup anchors
```{r dedup-whitelist}
message(" Step 3b: Deduplicating whitelisted anchors ")

DEDUP_THRESHOLD <- opt$dedup

anchor_sample_dist_wl <- distmat[whitelisted, sample_ids, drop = FALSE]

sample_cover <- lapply(sample_ids, function(s) {
  dists <- anchor_sample_dist_wl[, s]
  whitelisted[!is.na(dists) & dists <= opt$threshold]
})
names(sample_cover) <- sample_ids

kept_anchors    <- character(0)
dropped_anchors <- character(0)
dropped_by      <- character(0)

aa_dist <- distmat[whitelisted, whitelisted, drop = FALSE]

for (a in whitelisted) {
  if (length(kept_anchors) == 0) {
    kept_anchors <- c(kept_anchors, a)
    next
  }

  dists_to_kept <- aa_dist[a, kept_anchors]
  too_similar   <- any(!is.na(dists_to_kept) & dists_to_kept <= DEDUP_THRESHOLD)

  if (!too_similar) {
    kept_anchors <- c(kept_anchors, a)
    next
  }

  samples_it_covers <- names(sample_cover)[sapply(sample_cover, function(cv) a %in% cv)]
  sole_cover <- any(sapply(samples_it_covers, function(s) {
    remaining_cover <- sum(setdiff(kept_anchors, a) %in% sample_cover[[s]])
    current_cover_count <- length(sample_cover[[s]])
    remaining_cover < min(3L, current_cover_count)
  }))

  if (sole_cover) {
    kept_anchors <- c(kept_anchors, a)
    message(sprintf("  Kept   %s (sole cover for a sample)", a))
  } else {
    closest         <- kept_anchors[which.min(dists_to_kept)]
    dropped_anchors <- c(dropped_anchors, a)
    dropped_by      <- c(dropped_by, closest)
    message(sprintf("  Dropped %s (dist = %.4f to %s)", a, min(dists_to_kept, na.rm = TRUE), closest))
  }
}

message(sprintf("  Whitelisted before dedup: %d", length(whitelisted)))
message(sprintf("  Whitelisted after  dedup: %d", length(kept_anchors)))
message(sprintf("  Dropped:                  %d", length(dropped_anchors)))

if (length(dropped_anchors) > 0) {
  dedup_log <- data.frame(dropped_anchor = dropped_anchors,
                          kept_anchor    = dropped_by,
                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  write.csv(dedup_log, file.path(opt$output, "dedup_log.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  message("  Saved: dedup_log.csv")
}

# Update whitelisted — non_whitelisted is unchanged
whitelisted <- kept_anchors
```


# Steps 4 & 5: PD Subsetting (if needed)
```{r pd-subsetting}
# PD subsetting always runs on ALL anchors.
# Whitelisted anchors are always retained.
# Non-whitelisted anchors fill remaining slots (up to opt$subset total).
# If whitelisted alone >= opt$subset, we keep all whitelisted (may exceed opt$subset).

message(" Step 4: ML tree for PD subsetting ")
pd_ids  <- c(anchor_ids, sample_ids)   # ALL anchors, not just whitelisted
pd_aln  <- subset(aln, pd_ids)
pd_tree <- run_fasttree(pd_aln, "pd_subset", opt$output, opt$fasttree_bin)
pd_tree <- midpoint(pd_tree)

message(" Step 5: Greedy Faith's PD subsetting ")

n_whitelisted <- length(whitelisted)
slots_for_nonwl <- max(0L, opt$subset - n_whitelisted)

message(sprintf("  Whitelisted anchors (always kept): %d", n_whitelisted))
message(sprintf("  Slots available for non-whitelisted anchors: %d", slots_for_nonwl))

if (slots_for_nonwl == 0 && length(non_whitelisted) > 0) {
  message(sprintf(
    "  Whitelisted (%d) already meets or exceeds subset (%d); no non-whitelisted anchors added.",
    n_whitelisted, opt$subset
  ))
  final_anchors <- whitelisted
} else if (length(non_whitelisted) == 0 || slots_for_nonwl == 0) {
  final_anchors <- whitelisted
} else {
  # Greedy PD to pick the best non-whitelisted anchors to fill remaining slots
  message(sprintf("  Selecting up to %d non-whitelisted anchors by greedy PD…",
                  slots_for_nonwl))

  fixed_tips <- c(sample_ids, whitelisted)   # treat whitelisted + samples as fixed

  # Seed: non-whitelisted anchor with greatest marginal PD given fixed_tips
  message("  Finding seed non-whitelisted anchor…")
  best_pd  <- -Inf
  best_tip <- NULL
  for (a in non_whitelisted) {
    pd <- faith_pd(pd_tree, c(fixed_tips, a))
    if (pd > best_pd) { best_pd <- pd; best_tip <- a }
  }
  selected_nonwl  <- best_tip
  remaining_nonwl <- setdiff(non_whitelisted, selected_nonwl)
  message(sprintf("  Seed: %s (PD = %.4f)", selected_nonwl, best_pd))

  # Greedy expansion
  while (length(selected_nonwl) < slots_for_nonwl && length(remaining_nonwl) > 0) {
    best_pd   <- -Inf
    best_tip  <- NULL
    curr_tips <- c(fixed_tips, selected_nonwl)

    for (cand in remaining_nonwl) {
      pd <- faith_pd(pd_tree, c(curr_tips, cand))
      if (pd > best_pd) { best_pd <- pd; best_tip <- cand }
    }
    selected_nonwl  <- c(selected_nonwl, best_tip)
    remaining_nonwl <- setdiff(remaining_nonwl, best_tip)

    if (length(selected_nonwl) %% 10 == 0 || length(selected_nonwl) == slots_for_nonwl) {
      message(sprintf("  Selected %d / %d non-whitelisted  PD = %.4f",
                      length(selected_nonwl), slots_for_nonwl, best_pd))
    }
  }

  final_anchors <- c(whitelisted, selected_nonwl)
}

# Annotate final anchors with whitelist status (used in metadata output)
anchor_whitelist_status <- setNames(
  ifelse(final_anchors %in% whitelisted, "whitelisted", "non_whitelisted"),
  final_anchors
)

message(sprintf("  Final anchors: %d total (%d whitelisted, %d non-whitelisted)",
                length(final_anchors),
                sum(anchor_whitelist_status == "whitelisted"),
                sum(anchor_whitelist_status == "non_whitelisted")))
message(sprintf("  Final PD (anchors + samples): %.4f",
                faith_pd(pd_tree, c(sample_ids, final_anchors))))
```


# Step 6: Final ML Tree

```{r final-tree}
message(" Step 6: Final ML tree ")
final_ids  <- c(final_anchors, sample_ids)
final_aln  <- subset(aln, final_ids)
final_tree <- run_fasttree(final_aln, "final", opt$output, opt$fasttree_bin)
final_tree <- midpoint(final_tree)
```



# Step 7: Write Outputs
```{r write-outputs}
message(" Step 7: Writing outputs ")

# Newick trees
if (!is.null(pd_tree)) {
  pd_tree_path <- file.path(opt$output, "pd_subset_tree.nwk")
  write.tree(pd_tree, file = pd_tree_path)
  message(sprintf("  pd_subset_tree.nwk  (%d tips)", length(pd_tree$tip.label)))
}

final_tree_path <- file.path(opt$output, "final_tree.nwk")
write.tree(final_tree, file = final_tree_path)
message(sprintf("  final_tree.nwk      (%d tips)", length(final_tree$tip.label)))

# Unaligned FASTA (gaps stripped)
final_fasta_path <- file.path(opt$output, "final_subset.fasta")
final_dnabin     <- del.gaps(as.DNAbin(subset(aln, final_ids)))
write.FASTA(final_dnabin, file = final_fasta_path)
message(sprintf("  final_subset.fasta  (%d sequences, gaps stripped)", length(final_ids)))

# Aligned FASTA
final_aligned_path <- file.path(opt$output, "final_subset_aligned.fasta")
write.FASTA(as.DNAbin(subset(aln, final_ids)), file = final_aligned_path)
message(sprintf("  final_subset_aligned.fasta  (%d sequences)", length(final_ids)))

# Subset distance matrix
subset_dist_path <- file.path(opt$output, "final_subset_distance_matrix.csv")
write.csv(as.matrix(distmat[final_ids, final_ids]), subset_dist_path)
message(sprintf("  final_subset_distance_matrix.csv  (%d x %d)",
                length(final_ids), length(final_ids)))

# Tip metadata CSV (for ggtree %<+%)
# Merge base metadata, then add whitelist_status column
tip_meta <- merge(
  data.frame(label = final_tree$tip.label, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  meta[, c("label", "type", "dataset")],
  by = "label", all.x = TRUE
)

# Add whitelist_status: "whitelisted" / "non_whitelisted" for anchors, NA for samples
tip_meta$whitelist_status <- ifelse(
  tip_meta$label %in% names(anchor_whitelist_status),
  anchor_whitelist_status[tip_meta$label],
  NA_character_
)

meta_path <- file.path(opt$output, "tip_metadata.csv")
write.csv(tip_meta, meta_path, row.names = FALSE)
message(sprintf("  tip_metadata.csv    (%d rows)", nrow(tip_meta)))
```



# Report

```{r report}
report_lines <- c(
  "phylo_anchor_filter.Rmd — Report",
  strrep("=", 50),
  "",
  sprintf("FASTA:                  %s", opt$fasta),
  sprintf("Metadata:               %s", opt$metadata),
  sprintf("Distance model:         %s", opt$distance),
  sprintf("Threshold:              %.4f", opt$threshold),
  sprintf("Min overlap (bp):       %s",
          if (!is.null(opt$min_overlap)) opt$min_overlap else "off"),
  sprintf("Requested subset size:  %d", opt$subset),
  "",
  sprintf("Total anchors:          %d", length(anchor_ids)),
  sprintf("Total samples:          %d", length(sample_ids)),
  sprintf("Anchors whitelisted:    %d", length(whitelisted)),
  sprintf("Anchors non-whitelisted (kept): %d", length(non_whitelisted)),
  sprintf("Final anchors selected: %d", length(final_anchors)),
  sprintf("  of which whitelisted: %d", sum(anchor_whitelist_status == "whitelisted")),
  sprintf("  of which non-whitelisted: %d", sum(anchor_whitelist_status == "non_whitelisted")),
  sprintf("Final tree tips:        %d", length(final_tree$tip.label)),
  "",
  "Whitelisted anchor IDs:",
  paste(" ", whitelisted, collapse = "\n"),
  "",
  "Non-whitelisted anchor IDs in final set:",
  paste(" ", names(anchor_whitelist_status)[anchor_whitelist_status == "non_whitelisted"],
        collapse = "\n")
)
report_path <- file.path(opt$output, "report.txt")
writeLines(report_lines, con = report_path)
```

```{r done}
message("\nDone.")
```
